violations.text: Raw animal foods (such as tuna) were observed stored in the same container of ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked chicken) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as cooked chicken during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Sushi was observed cooling in the sushi display case. Sushi display case is inadequate for cooling. Per person in charge, sushi was prepared about 1.5 hours prior to inspection. Sushi was transferred to the freezer to rapidly cool. Sushi was cooled to 31-35 degrees Fahrenheit within 20 minutes of transfer. Establishment must not use sushi display case to cool food. Inspector called manager and manger said sushi is supposed to be cooled in the freezer or walk-in cooler and that they will not cool in the sushi display case. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) seafood (tuna, imitation crab shredded, and imitation crab sticks) was observed thawing with the package intact. ROP seafood must be removed from the package when thawing. Food was observed held at 27-36 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler at time of inspection. Food was removed from the package at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/17/2026
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Application printed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/02/2024
